programs.label: Foundations
programs.title: Breaking the Comfort Mindset to Self Management
programs.tagline: Entry-level outdoor conditioning for people stepping into the mountain environment for the first time.
programs.entryStandard: Basic fitness required. Ability to walk 4-6 km with a light pack. No prior trekking experience needed. Higher BMI is acceptable if functional fitness is adequate.
programs.summary: Foundations is the entry point into the Mountain Pro system. It introduces participants to an outdoor operating mindset and gradually removes psychological dependency on comfort.
programs.outcome: Develop self-management, outdoor confidence, and the first layer of four-dimensional fitness.
trainingObjectives: Understanding physical, mental, social, and spiritual fitness

chat.speaker: System
chat.text: Mountain Pro Foundations is the entry level of the progression.

chat.speaker: Mentor
chat.text: The program introduces participants to the mountain environment as a structured training ground rather than a recreational setting.

chat.speaker: Trainee
chat.text: The initial benchmark is not distance alone, but the ability to manage effort, discomfort, and self-reliance in outdoor conditions.

chat.speaker: Mentor
chat.text: Key outcomes include composure, movement discipline, and confidence while functioning in natural terrain.

programs.label: Practitioner
programs.title: From Individual Capability to Team Efficiency
programs.tagline: A team-oriented mountain program that builds endurance, reliability, and coordinated movement in uneven terrain.
programs.entryStandard: Ability to cover 8-10 km in 2-3 hours with an 8-10 kg ruck and move continuously on uneven terrain with short recovery breaks.
programs.summary: At this level the difficulty increases and the emphasis shifts from self-management to operating effectively as part of a team in the mountains.
programs.outcome: Build endurance, team discipline, and decision-making under physical stress.

chat.speaker: System
chat.text: Mountain Pro Practitioner develops capability beyond the individual level.

chat.speaker: Mentor
chat.text: At this stage, individual fitness must translate into team efficiency, coordination, and shared reliability.

chat.speaker: Trainee
chat.text: Participants are expected to maintain pace, communication quality, and sound decision-making as physical stress increases.

chat.speaker: Mentor
chat.text: Longer movements and more demanding terrain are used to assess whether participants can contribute effectively under pressure without disrupting team rhythm.

programs.label: High Altitude Operations
programs.title: Leadership and Operating in Harsh Mountain Environments
programs.tagline: Built for participants preparing to function and lead when reduced oxygen, fatigue, and harsh weather reshape the fight.
programs.entryStandard: Ability to sustain 10-15 km over 4-6 hours with a 10-15 kg load. Resting HR recovery should normalize within 2-3 minutes after moderate exertion. Prior trekking experience recommended.
programs.summary: This stage introduces participants to the challenges of high altitude environments where reduced oxygen, harsh weather, and fatigue become major factors.
programs.outcome: Operate efficiently and lead small teams under physical and environmental stress. Also highly recommended for BMC aspirants.

chat.speaker: System
chat.text: High Altitude Operations is designed for harsh, oxygen-limited mountain environments.

chat.speaker: Mentor
chat.text: At altitude, reduced oxygen, slower recovery, and environmental exposure all influence movement and judgement at the same time.

chat.speaker: Trainee
chat.text: The program therefore emphasizes movement control, environmental awareness, and leadership under sustained physical stress.

chat.speaker: Mentor
chat.text: This level prepares participants to remain capable when weather, terrain, and fatigue significantly reduce operational margin.

programs.label: Alpine Operator
programs.title: Complete Mountain Capability
programs.tagline: The highest level of Mountain Pro, built for serious terrain, technical mountaineering exposure, and leadership with full ownership.
programs.entryStandard: Ability to operate 6-8 hours daily for multiple days carrying 15-20 kg, including steep ascents, with consistent recovery and performance. Strong trekking background and excellent physical fitness required.
programs.summary: This is the highest level of the Mountain Pro system. Participants are introduced to technical mountaineering equipment and alpine-style operations where complete self-reliance is expected.
programs.outcome: Develop alpine self-sufficiency, technical competence, and leadership in serious mountain terrain. Also highly recommended for AMC aspirants.

chat.speaker: System
chat.text: Alpine Operator is the highest level in the Mountain Pro system.

chat.speaker: Mentor
chat.text: The focus shifts from participation to complete competence, ownership, and the ability to operate with technical intent.

chat.speaker: Trainee
chat.text: Participants are expected to plan, lead, and remain accountable while operating in serious mountain terrain.

chat.speaker: Mentor
chat.text: Alpine Operator brings self-reliance, technical mountaineering skill, and leadership together as one complete capability system.

mentorshipPoints: Mentorship treks bridge the gap between learning isolated skills and building real mountain capability.
Climbs are conducted in true alpine style: lean, efficient, and focused on judgement, responsibility, and self-reliance.
Participants take on roles, make calls, and operate in real environments while being observed, corrected, and refined.
Over time this process builds confidence to lead, composure under pressure, and instinct for risk assessment.
